qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: GraphQL handler reads unbounded HTTP request body enabling memory-exhaustion DoS
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes malicious p2p message causing high CPU usage through KZG verification in the transaction fetcher (eth/fetcher/tx_fetcher.go). This finding describes unbounded HTTP body reading in the GraphQL HTTP endpoint (graphql/service.go:48). Different vulnerable component (HTTP GraphQL handler vs p2p transaction fetcher), different attack vector (HTTP vs p2p), different root cause (missing body size limit vs missing KZG verification error handling), and different DoS mechanism (memory exhaustion vs CPU exhaustion).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The advisory describes a DoS vulnerability in go-ethereum caused by malicious p2p blob transactions triggering expensive KZG verification operations without proper peer termination (fixed in commit abeb78c). The single qualifying finding describes GraphQL HTTP endpoint memory exhaustion from unbounded request body reading, which is unrelated to the advisory root cause. The finding does not identify the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause in eth/fetcher/tx_fetcher.go and core/txpool/validation.go where malicious blob transaction processing lacks defensive KZG verification error handling.
evidence: Advisory summary: 'go-ethereum is vulnerable to high CPU usage leading to DoS via malicious p2p message'
Patch commit abeb78c shows fixes in eth/fetcher/tx_fetcher.go adding protocol violation handling for ErrKZGVerificationError
Patch commit shows core/txpool/validation.go wrapping KZG verification errors as ErrKZGVerificationError
Patch commit shows crypto/ecies/ecies.go fixing message length validation
Finding graphql-body-size-dos targets graphql/service.go:48 (HTTP endpoint, not p2p)
No overlap in vulnerable component, attack vector, or root-cause mechanism
